body.u: As I sat looking out of the window of the old people’s home, I watched the young teenagers coming home from school. How smart they looked in their navy blue uniforms. It made me remember my schooldays, I was just sixteen years old when I left. At that time my main ambition was to get a job and earn some money, so that I could buy and do all things that I had wished for.

Luckily, due to a lot of help from my father I became a clerical assistant in the civil service at Chatham dockyard. My father and brother were both working theirthere at the time. I started on thirty pounds, I thought this was pretty good money, well it seemed to be then. I had great fun as a teenager. As I my money rose, the better the time I had. At seventeen my family and I moved off the island nearer to Chatham. I went out most nights of the week. Occasionally during the summer I would spend weekends at Sheppey staying with friends and they would come back to stay with me. The best bit came when I was eighteen. I got a really smart car, well I thought it was, really it was an old banger but I did it up and hit the road. From then I really got round, went to London with my mates, shopingshopping and going to concerts etc.

The next biggest step was when I met Steve, I fell for him a few months after being introduced. We started going out together and eventually got engaged. The engagement lasted for six months and we got married when I was twenty two. It was a fabulous white wedding and we honeymooned in the South of France. We had a small but nice house which we saved the deposit for before marrying. I had my first child at twenty five, a little boy called Danny. He was a proper lad, always in the mud or fighting and playing football. At twenty eight I had a girl which we called Kelly. With the children we just didn’t have the money to go out like we used to especially since I had given up work. Steve was earning about eighty pound a week and we didn’t do too bad.

Life didn’t change a great deal again until my own children left home, except that gradually we got better off. I was nearly fifty when both had left home. Kelly got married and Danny lived single for about a year, then married. After that Steve and I were lonely but soon began to enjoy life in the way we did before having children.
“Steve, your home early, I’ll get the dinner ready”.
